personality_one_liner: A high-contrast, tactical night-patrol command center built for speed, glare reduction, and field operational clarity.

spacing.rule: Generous tap targets for one-handed use, but compact vertical rhythms to prioritize glanceability of operational data.

dont's: No blur or glass on field operational tools - clarity and contrast beat aesthetics
Do not use glass for headers or bottom sheets in this app

screens.name: Shift Login
screens.purpose: Guard identifies themselves, sets shift date, and selects the manager handling diversions before starting patrol.

states.error: Error fetching managers or saving shift data.

screens.layout: Utilitarian form structure. Large text inputs for Security Number and Name. A horizontally scrollable list or a set of large radio-style cards for 'Manager Diversion Selector' (Name + Mobile). A sticky bottom CTA 'Start Shift' sitting outside the scroll view so it's always accessible.

screens.name: Patrol Dashboard
screens.purpose: A live operational command center showing sync status, current shift info, and a feed of logged entries.

states.empty: Full-screen 'night sky' illustration with 'No logs yet. Start your patrol.' and a primary CTA.
states.error: Connection offline message (expected in field operations, emphasize local save instead of error).

screens.layout: Top persistent stats bar showing 'Offline Mode: Syncing...' or 'Synced', and active manager. Main area is a chronological list of logged checkpoints with timestamps. A large sticky FAB or bottom sticky primary CTA 'Log Patrol Entry'. Use warm brand amber accents for active states.

screens.name: Log Entry Form
screens.purpose: Fast data entry for each patrol checkpoint with automatic timestamping and attachment options.

states.error: Failed to get GPS location -> 'Retry GPS' button.

screens.layout: Auto-locked timestamp at the top. Single-column form: 'Location' input, 'Action Taken' large text area with an inline Voice-to-Text icon button. Two large, tactile action buttons (half-width grid) for 'Capture GPS' and 'Attach Photo'. Thumb-zone sticky CTA 'Save Entry' at bottom.

screens.name: Shift Summary & Submit
screens.purpose: Review the full night's logs, export to PDF, and launch email app to submit.
screens.layout: A summary card with total patrols and shift duration. Below it, a final list of entries. A sticky bottom container with two actions: a secondary button for 'Export PDF' and a primary button 'Submit Nightly Report'.

empty_state_night.alt: Dark night sky with clouds and stars, representing the after-hours patrol environment

location_tent.alt: Camping tent glowing in the dark night

instructions_to_main_agent: Prioritize one-handed usage: buttons must be 44x44pt minimum, with primary CTAs strictly pinned to the bottom of the screen above the safe area.
Do NOT use glassmorphism. Use solid surfaceSecondary or surfaceTertiary to maintain high-contrast readability in field environments.
Implement the 'Dark-First Utility' aesthetic: #101112 backgrounds with amber (#F5A623) accents. Do not use pure black (#000000) for large surfaces to avoid harsh contrast fatigue.
Ensure 'Offline Sync' is a prominent status indicator. It should feel robust, letting the user know their data is safe locally.
For the Manager Diversion Selector, use a scrollable list of chunky, tactile radio cards rather than standard dropdowns.
Include a gradient scrim when placing text over the location photo attachments to guarantee WCAG compliance.
The Voice-to-Text action should be prominently placed inside or right next to the 'Action Taken' text area.
Ensure the GPS capture locks the coordinates and displays a success state clearly.
